name: El Roquez Loop
description: An easy 12.9-mile (20.7 km) mountain biking route near Chirivel, Spain, winding through scenic olive groves with 497 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Chirivel offers a diverse landscape characterized by varied elevations and open terrain. The region features routes that traverse through agricultural areas, past small villages, and into the foothills of the Sierra de María. Mountain bike trails in Chirivel provide opportunities to explore the natural environment, with some routes offering panoramic viewpoints. Best mountain bike trails around Chirivel The most popular mountain bike trail is El Roquez Loop, an easy 12.9 miles (20.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This route offers gentle gradients through open terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Santuario del Saliente – Santuario del Saliente loop from Santuario del Saliente, a moderate 26.0 miles (41.8 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain with views of the surrounding hills. Local mountain bikers also love the Views of the Sierra de María – Puntal del Morral Viewpoint loop from Área recreativa La Piza, a 27.5 miles (44.3 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes with a viewpoint, often completed in about 4 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Chirivel is defined by open landscapes, varied elevations, and views of mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more advanced mountain biking routes. The routes in Chirivel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 130 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Chirivel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Chirivel?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Chirivel, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Chirivel?
A: Yes, Chirivel offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031305047/el-roquez-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Roquez Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 12.9-mile (20.7 km) route with gentle gradients through open terrain.

Q: What about more challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Chirivel has several difficult routes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Sierra de María – Puntal del Morral Viewpoint loop from Área recreativa La Piza&lt;/a&gt;, a 27.5-mile (44.3 km) trail through mountainous landscapes with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;María Village – View of Sierra de María loop from Chirivel&lt;/a&gt;, covering 30.8 miles (49.6 km) with over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails with scenic viewpoints in Chirivel?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Chirivel offer panoramic viewpoints, especially those venturing into the Sierra de María. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Sierra de María – Puntal del Morral Viewpoint loop from Área recreativa La Piza&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase stunning vistas, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2074254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sierra de María&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Chirivel?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Chirivel are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031305047/el-roquez-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Roquez Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31471385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Saliente – Santuario del Saliente loop from Santuario del Saliente&lt;/a&gt;, and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélez-Blanco – Vélez-Rubio loop from Chirivel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Chirivel?
A: Mountain bike trails around Chirivel feature a diverse landscape with varied elevations and open terrain. You'll traverse through agricultural areas, past small villages, and into the foothills of the Sierra de María, encountering both gentle gradients and more challenging mountainous sections.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore near the mountain bike trails in Chirivel?
A: Beyond the trails, Chirivel offers several points of interest. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2580445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Saliente&lt;/a&gt;, explore the charming town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélez-Rubio&lt;/a&gt;, or discover natural wonders like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6134128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Inscriptions&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7263595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Umbría de la Virgen Botanical Garden&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Chirivel?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Chirivel is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the weather is generally more stable, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can bring colder temperatures, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are mountain bike trails in Chirivel dog-friendly?
A: While many trails in natural areas are generally accessible, it's always recommended to check specific route details on komoot for any restrictions or local regulations regarding dogs. When riding with your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry enough water for both of you.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Chirivel?
A: The mountain biking experience in Chirivel is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the open landscapes, and the stunning views of the surrounding mountain ranges, particularly the Sierra de María.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Chirivel?
A: For families looking for a relaxed ride, the easier trails around Chirivel are a good starting point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031305047/el-roquez-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Roquez Loop&lt;/a&gt;, with its gentle gradients and open terrain, is suitable for families with some biking experience, offering a pleasant way to explore the area together.
tips: The path between Chirivel and the top of El Roquez runs through a fantastic and picturesque landscape of olive trees.

This small town is located at an altitude of 1,034 meters in the region of Los Vélez, Almería. The name comes from the Latin word silvella, which means little forest. The origin of the current municipality of a group of houses of the manor of Los Vélez, when in 1859 it achieved its independence from Vélez Rubio. In any case, the archaeological remains found seem to indicate that since prehistoric times there would already be settlements in the same place.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The El Roquez — circular desde Chirivel route is designed for an easy pace, and most riders complete it in approximately 1 hour and 40 minutes. This duration can vary based on your personal speed and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! This route is explicitly rated as easy and is perfect for beginners, families, or anyone looking for a relaxed ride. The terrain is mostly smooth and non-technical, requiring no advanced riding skills.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the El Roquez circular route?
faqs.answer: You'll be riding through a fantastic and scenic landscape, primarily on agricultural paths or well-maintained dirt tracks. The route is known for its beautiful rural scenery, especially the extensive olive groves, offering mostly smooth and non-technical surfaces.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail near Chirivel?
faqs.answer: While the specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, as a circular route starting and ending near Chirivel, you can typically find convenient parking within or on the outskirts of Chirivel Village. Look for public parking areas that accommodate vehicles for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the El Roquez mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region, especially on rural paths. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect local wildlife and agricultural areas. Please also remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to enjoy this route is during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summers can be very hot in Almería, and winters might bring cooler temperatures, though riding is often possible year-round.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay an entrance fee to ride the El Roquez — circular desde Chirivel route. It's freely accessible for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful rural scenery, including a picturesque Path Among Olive Trees. You'll also experience the charm of Chirivel Village, which serves as both the start and end point of this circular journey.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You'll find yourself riding along parts of the Rambla de Chirivel and the Camino de Aspilla a Los Álamos, as well as the Camino de La Cumbre, which contribute to the varied experience of the circular trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its easy difficulty and moderate length, we recommend bringing plenty of water, some snacks, a basic bike repair kit, and sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses). A helmet is always essential for mountain biking, and comfortable clothing suitable for the weather is advised.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Chirivel Village, you'll find local cafes, bars, and restaurants there where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's a great opportunity to experience local Spanish cuisine.
